reviewer: hand-reviewed (Opus) — headless first-visit + warm-cache runs, real cosine matrix, CLS-pooling ranking check, desktop light/dark screenshots, responsive matrix

rubric.evidence: Headless first-visit run (WASM, no GPU): clicked Download (~150 MB) → state 'ready' → clicked Embed → the cosine matrix rendered 36 real cells (6×6). Standalone CLS-pooling run: related pair cos 0.645 vs unrelated 0.411 — the relevant passage ranks first. 0 console errors. Real Alibaba-NLP/gte-modernbert-base feature-extraction pipeline runs in models/gte-modernbert-embeddings/worker.js.
rubric.notes: ModernBERT backbone genuinely loads and runs under transformers.js 3.7.5 (WASM q8). CLS pooling (the model's trained pooling) verified to separate related from unrelated.

rubric.evidence: Output shape matches feature-extraction: one 768-d vector per input (readout shows dims 768). 'See inside' renders the real vector strip + pre-norm magnitude + token count / 8192. The similarity matrix diagonal is 1.00 and off-diagonal cosines are in [-1,1].
rubric.notes: Correct pooling honesty: pooling:'cls', normalize:false in the worker, then L2-normalize in JS so cosine = dot product.

rubric.evidence: Desktop screenshot confirms the at-a-glance table: model Alibaba-NLP/gte-modernbert-base linked, task feature-extraction, backbone ModernBERT-base (~149M) → 768-d, context length 8192, pooling 'CLS token' (contrast GTE-small mean), quantization q8, backend WASM, size ~150 MB, license Apache-2.0. inference-off-main-thread via worker.js.
rubric.notes: The three distinctions from other embedders — ModernBERT backbone, 8192 context, CLS pooling — are all stated and accurate.

rubric.evidence: Fresh profile: state 'download-required', single action 'Download model (~150 MB)' — no silent auto-download. Warm reload (same browser, cache persisted): state reached 'ready' automatically with ZERO download buttons present. Uses the shared createModelLoader.
rubric.notes: Matches the auto-init contract: absent → labelled Download; current cached → auto-init, no button. The multi-model rung uses two independent loaders (embedder + reranker).

rubric.evidence: role=status aria-live regions on every page; loader announces checking→downloading→ready with a progress bar during the ~150 MB download; a .status.err surfaces embedding/rerank failures on the page.
rubric.notes: Offline-cached and partial-eviction paths are handled by the shared loader but not separately driven here.

rubric.evidence: Real, labelled controls across the ladder: passages textarea, Embed/Reset/Search buttons, query input (Enter bound), example-pair buttons (basics), duplicate-threshold + k + top-k range sliders with <output> readouts, cross-encoder compare button. All native, keyboard-operable.
rubric.notes: A full driven keyboard-traversal pass at both viewports is the remaining agent check; the search top-hit render was not captured in the automated window (timing) but uses the same verified embed+cosine path as the matrix.

rubric.evidence: On a fresh profile the matrix/search/inspect areas are empty and the loader shows the honest download state — nothing canned is presented as live output. The 36-cell matrix and the model-vs-model comparison come from real on-device runs.

conformance script assertions pass by construction: every button has a text name; no without alt. The similarity matrix is a real with scope=col/row headers + per-cell title; the PCA scatter SVG has role=img + aria-label; sliders have associated labels + .
rubric.notes: Matrix cell contrast uses accent-ink on high-similarity cells to stay legible in both themes.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check.mjs: desktop (1280×800) and mobile (360×740 DPR3) both automated-clean — scrollWidth ≤ innerWidth, no clipped controls, console clean, network clean. Screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/gte-modernbert-embeddings/{desktop,mobile}.png. The matrix is wrapped in .matrix-scroll (overflow-x:auto) and grid-2 collapses to one column.
rubric.notes: support kept at needs-review (honest) pending a driven interaction pass; layout parity itself is clean, and the wide matrix scrolls inside its own container rather than overflowing the page.

rubric.evidence: All embedding + reranking inference runs in Web Workers off the main thread. Main-thread maths (cosine, power-iteration PCA-2D, spherical k-means, union-free) runs over a handful of already-computed 768-d vectors, cheap enough to stay responsive.
rubric.notes: Architecture is INP-friendly; an explicit INP measurement during Embed would confirm.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy synchronous work off the main thread (workers) / yield so it doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — feature-extraction runs in models/gte-modernbert-embeddings/worker.js; the multi-model rung adds the built bge-reranker worker; only light vector maths runs on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; first-visit run stayed responsive during the ~150 MB download and matrix render

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use grid repeat(auto-fit,minmax()), flex-wrap and overflow-x:auto on wide content to avoid page-level horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — grid-2 uses minmax(300px,1fr) auto-fit; the wide similarity matrix scrolls inside .matrix-scroll rather than overflowing the body; slider rows wrap.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce load/inference failures in a live region the user can read, not only the console.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — embedding/search/rerank failures set a .status.err in a role=status aria-live region; the shared loader exposes a Retry path.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: rowStatus/status role=status aria-live in every page; loader error state

openQuestions: Drive a full keyboard traversal at mobile + desktop: are all sliders, the Embed/Search/Compare buttons and the passage/query fields reachable in a sensible focus order with a visible focus ring, and do <output> values announce?
Capture the search ranking render in-UI (the automated window missed it on timing) to add a screenshot of the top hit for the io-shape row.
How much does q8 quantisation cost GTE-ModernBERT's MTEB-level retrieval quality vs fp16? Worth a small evidence pass on the retrieve→rerank rung.

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass for gte-modernbert-embeddings: drive every slider/button/input on mobile+desktop, capture the search + rerank output, then flip support to ok (or unsupported+evidence).
Add a 'long-context needle' wild demo: embed one very long document (near 8192 tokens) and show retrieval finding a fact buried deep in it, contrasting a 512-token window model that must chunk.

summary: gte-modernbert-embeddings is a strong, honest demo of a distinct embedder: real feature-extraction via transformers.js in a Web Worker on the ModernBERT backbone (8192-token context, CLS pooling, 768-d), verified to load and produce a real cosine matrix and to rank relevant above unrelated. Accurate at-a-glance disclosure of the three distinctions from the other built embedders, honest absent-model Download state (no silent ~150 MB transfer), verified warm-cache auto-init with no button, clean mobile+desktop layout with the wide matrix scrolling in its own container, and a four-rung ladder (basics similarity → practical long-doc search + dedup → wild k-means clustering → multi-model retrieve-then-rerank with the built bge cross-encoder). Remaining work is a driven interaction pass before flipping support from needs-review to ok. Revision 1.
